title: Gooey Comfortable Russian Flaxseed Stir-fry
description: A floral, gooey, comforting Russian dish featuring Chinese sesame sauce, caramelized flaxseeds, and a dairy-free-friendly twist.

ingredients.name: Flaxseeds
ingredients.notes: Soaked in water for 30 minutes before cooking to improve texture and flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Crushed finely for depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Onions
ingredients.notes: Chopped finely, used for sweetness and base flavor.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Sliced thinly for quick cooking time.

ingredients.name: Pork Slices (or substitute with tofu or tempeh)
ingredients.notes: Cooked to golden brown, ensuring no raw pork in the dish.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional for added umami and texture.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: To help caramelize the flaxseeds and onions.

instructions: Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add a small amount of olive oil if preferred, then add flaxseeds, onions, and carrots. Stir-fry until the onions are translucent and the vegetables start to soften.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant but not browned.
Mix in pork slices (or substitute) along with any optional soy sauce, ensuring they’re evenly coated by the sauces.
In a small bowl, mix Chinese sesame sauce with water. Slowly pour this mixture into the wok while continuously stirring to prevent sticking and create a glossy coating on the vegetables and meats.
Continue cooking until all ingredients are heated through and nicely caramelized, adjusting seasonings as necessary to taste.
Serve hot over a bed of steamed rice or noodles for an authentic Russian experience.
